html:
Exclusive to:

GenericHeldLetting Go

The Personal Floater is an item exclusive to Don't Starve Together. If present in the player's inventory or backpack when they fall into Ocean, the Personal Floater will allow the player to float back up to the water surface, giving them 20 Wetness at first and gradually rising the wetness meter every 1.5 seconds. While in this state, the player won't be able to craft, attack or access their backpack, but will still be able to equip/unequip armors, sort items in their inventory and consume Food. The player can choose either the "Let Go" option to drown (and take the regular drowning penalties), place down a Boat, or get back up onto any nearby Boats or Shore, leaving the Personal Floater behind in the water to be picked back up. The floater takes up the player item's slot, therefore making any Tools or Weapons unusable. If a Mob attacks the player while they're floating, it will make them dismount the Floater and drown instead.

While floating, the Survivors can swim very slowly for 3 seconds until they get tired for a second. The swimming speed is not affected by speed altering equipment like a Magiluminescence or character-specific perks like Wolfgang's Mightiness or Wurt's Amphibian likeness.

Survivors can't use their items and their ability (like Wortox's Soul Hop) while in their floating state; for Woodie, his Wereform (except for Weregoose form) will still drown even with the Floater. Character's visual transformation like Wolfgang or Wanda won't play the animation, instead will cut to their corresponding form immediately.

Contents

  • 1 Skins
  • 2 Tips
  • 3 Trivia
  • 4 Assets
  • 5 Gallery

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Loyal Cawnival Cushion Floateskin was originally awarded for playing Don't Starve Together between July 3, 2025, the release day of the Hostile Takeover patch 677386.
Loyal
Cawnival Collection
Cawnival Cushion Floater
Take the festivities to the open water - comfortably afloat.

Tips

  • Any item held in hand before drowning will fall into the water, but otherwise, no other item will fall out of the player's inventory (unless the player "Lets Go" and drowns).
  • If the player has more than 1 floater in their inventory or backpack, "Letting Go" will use another floater(s) before actually drowning.
  • Having a Personal Floater while wearing a Lazy Forager will cause the item to be retrieved back automatically, preventing the player from losing the item and, in some cases, save them from drowning twice if a Mob was attacking. It's ideal to carry two of them to be safe from Drowning, but be cautious of the risk of having too much Wetness (except for Wurt, who benefits from it).
  • Darkness can be dangerous for a stranded player, due to the inability to use items or craft, and a single hit from the Darkness will cause the player to let go and drown.
  • The Personal Floater is an easy way to gain wetness without any risk.

Trivia

  • The Personal Floater was added in the Hostile Takeover update.
    • When it firstly added to the game, it works similarly to Wes's Inflatable Vest.
  • Wes has his own miming animation while holding onto a Personal Floater.
  • WX-78 has a custom animation for letting go of the Personal Floater. This animation is a reference to the death of the Terminator from Terminator 2: Judgement Day.

Assets

Personal FloaterCawnival Cushion Floater

  • Dropped
  • Used

  • Dropped
  • Used

Gallery

  • Personal Floater Concept Art from Hostile Takeover update stream from Rhymes with Play.
  • Wes miming while on a Personal Floater.
  • WX-78 letting go a Personal Floater.

GenericHeldLetting Go
“Oh, buoyancy!”
–Wilson, when examining the Personal Floater
“Oh good, because I hate drowning.”
–Willow, when examining the Personal Floater
“Wolfgang exceptional swimmer, but keep just in case.”
–Wolfgang, when examining the Personal Floater
“One does not need water to drown.”
–Wendy, when examining the Personal Floater
“MINE”
–WX-78, when examining the Personal Floater
“A passive deployment flotation device. I approve.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining the Personal Floater
“There's room for both of us, Luce.”
–Woodie, when examining the Personal Floater
“Where does it say, "women and children first"?”
–Maxwell, when examining the Personal Floater
“Nöt this day, Njörd!”
–Wigfrid, when examining the Personal Floater
“But we're not afraid of the water!”
–Webber, when examining the Personal Floater
“It will save me from the brine!”
–Warly, when examining the Personal Floater
“No more scared biiig water”
–Wormwood, when examining the Personal Floater
“Sure beats drownin'.”
–Winona, when examining the Personal Floater
“Because there's no time to think, when you're plunged in the drink!”
–Wortox, when examining the Personal Floater
“Me best floater, florp!”
–Wurt, when examining the Personal Floater
“We love water safety! Don't we, Wobes?”
–Walter, when examining the Personal Floater
“How convenient!”
–Wanda, when examining the Personal Floater
“I was busy drowning but something came up... me!”
–Wilson, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Something touched my foot.”
–Willow, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Wolfgang unsinkable!”
–Wolfgang, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“What now?”
–Wendy, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“ERROR: WHY WHHHY!”
–WX-78, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Hmm, rather convenient.”
–Wickerbottom, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“That was a close one!”
–Woodie, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“There are unspeakable horrors below.”
–Maxwell, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Aye, I deny thee, Njörd!”
–Wigfrid, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“We float!”
–Webber, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“I feel like a dumpling!”
–Warly, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Good baby floater”
–Wormwood, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Thank goodness for this!”
–Winona, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“Wet fur? What for?”
–Wortox, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“See? Me best floater.”
–Wurt, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“I knew I'd come out on top!”
–Walter, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“I saw my life flash before my eyes... not in any particular order.”
–Wanda, after being saved by a Personal Floater.
“I hate being kept in susp-”
–Wilson, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“I hate waiting!”
–Willow, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Oopsie!”
–Wolfgang, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“It's time.”
–Wendy, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“I WILL RETURN”
–WX-78, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Time for a dip!”
–Wickerbottom, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Why'd I let-”
–Woodie, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Ah!”
–Maxwell, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Curse thee, Njörd!”
–Wigfrid, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Weeee!”
–Webber, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Was that wise?”
–Warly, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Bye, baby flo-”
–Wormwood, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Like a bag of rocks!”
–Winona, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“No time to rhy-”
–Wortox, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Glurph!”
–Wurt, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“Submerging!”
–Walter, after releasing the Personal Floater.
“I hate this part!”
–Wanda, after releasing the Personal Floater.
The Personal Floater is an item exclusive to Don't Starve Together. If present in the player's inventory or backpack when they fall into Ocean, the Personal Floater will allow the player to float back up to the water surface, giving them 20 Wetness at first and gradually rising the wetness meter every 1.5 seconds. While in this state, the player won't be able to craft, attack or access their backpack, but will still be able to equip/unequip armors, sort items in their inventory and consume Food. The floater takes up the player item's slot, therefore making any Tools or Weapons unusable. If a Mob attacks the player while they're floating, it will make them dismount the Floater and drown instead.
While floating, the Survivors can swim very slowly for 3 seconds until they get tired for a second. The swimming speed is not affected by speed altering equipment like a Magiluminescence or character-specific perks like Wolfgang's Mightiness or Wurt's Amphibian likeness.

The Loyal Cawnival Cushion Floateskin was originally awarded for playing Don't Starve Together between July 3, 2025, the release day of the Hostile Takeover patch 677386.

Tips[edit | edit source]
Any item held in hand before drowning will fall into the water, but otherwise, no other item will fall out of the player's inventory (unless the player "Lets Go" and drowns).
If the player has more than 1 floater in their inventory or backpack, "Letting Go" will use another floater(s) before actually drowning.
Having a Personal Floater while wearing a Lazy Forager will cause the item to be retrieved back automatically, preventing the player from losing the item and, in some cases, save them from drowning twice if a Mob was attacking. It's ideal to carry two of them to be safe from Drowning, but be cautious of the risk of having too much Wetness (except for Wurt, who benefits from it).

The Personal Floater is an easy way to gain wetness without any risk.

The Personal Floater was added in the Hostile Takeover update.
When it firstly added to the game, it works similarly to Wes's Inflatable Vest.
Wes has his own miming animation while holding onto a Personal Floater.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Phao cá nhân là một vật phẩm độc quyền của Don't Starve Together. Nếu có trong hành trang hoặc ba lô của người chơi khi họ rơi xuống Đại dương, Phao cá nhân sẽ cho phép người chơi nổi trở lại mặt nước, ban đầu cung cấp cho họ 20 Độ ẩm và tăng dần đồng hồ đo độ ẩm sau mỗi 1,5 giây. Khi ở trạng thái này, người chơi sẽ không thể chế tạo, tấn công hoặc lấy ba lô của mình, nhưng vẫn có thể trang bị/tháo áo giáp, sắp xếp vật phẩm trong kho và tiêu thụ Thực phẩm. Người chơi có thể chọn tùy chọn "Let Go" để chết đuối (và chết đuối thông thường…
